questions.question: Explain the eight components of a Reinforcement Learning system and describe how they interact with each other in the RL loop.
keyPoints: Define Agent: The learner/decision maker
Define Environment: External world for interaction
Define State: Current situation/configuration
Define Action: Choices available to agent
Define Reward: Immediate feedback signal
Define Policy: Mapping from states to actions
Define Value Function: Expected long-term rewards
Define Model: Representation of environment dynamics
Explain the interaction loop: Agent observes state → selects action → receives reward and new state → updates knowledge → repeats

questions.question: Differentiate between reward and value function in Reinforcement Learning.
keyPoints: Reward is immediate feedback from environment after an action
Value function estimates expected cumulative future rewards
Reward is one-time scalar; value is long-term estimate
Value helps in comparing states and planning future actions

questions.question: What is the difference between model-based and model-free Reinforcement Learning approaches?
keyPoints: Model-based uses a model of environment for planning
Model-free learns directly from experience without model
Model-based can simulate future scenarios
Model-free is simpler but may need more experience to learn

questions.question: Explain the concept of policy in Reinforcement Learning and distinguish between deterministic and stochastic policies.
keyPoints: Policy is the agent's behavior strategy
Policy maps states to actions
Deterministic policy: π(s) = a (specific action for each state)
Stochastic policy: π(a|s) = P(a|s) (probability distribution over actions)
Policies can be improved through learning based on rewards
